threats.title: Permanent Plot Locking via Missing State Update in Plot Transfer
threats.hypothesis_description: The transferToUnoccupiedPlot function fails to update the plotId in the toilerState mapping. When a user subsequently unstakes, the contract clears the old (already empty) plot and leaves the new plot permanently marked as occupied.
threats.investigation_rationale: This state desynchronization allows any renter to permanently brick a landlord's plots, destroying their ability to earn tax revenue and reducing the protocol's overall staking capacity.

proof_of_signal.pattern: plotOccupied is updated for new plotId, but toilerState[tokenId].plotId is never updated

preconditions: Attacker has at least one staked Munchable
Target landlord has at least one unoccupied plot

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Attacker transfers to plot B, unstake clears plot A, plot B remains occupied forever
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Griefing attack with zero cost to attacker beyond gas, causing 100% loss of future revenue for the targeted plot

economic_questions: Can this be used to extort landlords?
Is there any admin function to forcefully clear occupied plots?
validation_requirements: Verify plotOccupied state for the new plot after unstaking

preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Two blocks or same block if composed

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Any time a landlord has open plots
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Reduces total protocol staking capacity over time

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Loss of all future tax revenue for affected plots
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Entire protocol staking capacity could be bricked

threats.title: Systemic Reward Deflation via Incorrect Percentage Math
threats.hypothesis_description: The formula for applying bonuses to Schnibbles rewards divides the base reward by 100 instead of adding the bonus percentage to 100%. This results in all users receiving approximately 1% of their intended rewards.
threats.investigation_rationale: This is a fundamental mathematical defect in the core economic engine of the protocol, drastically altering the intended emission rate and breaking the game's economy.

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Calculates S * (1 + B) / 100 instead of S * (100 + B) / 100
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Systemic math error affecting all reward accrual
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Protocol issues 99% fewer rewards than intended, destroying player incentives

economic_questions: Does this affect both renter and landlord rewards equally?
Are there any configurations of finalBonus that can offset this?
validation_requirements: Calculate expected vs actual rewards with finalBonus = 0 and finalBonus = 10
Verify the impact on total protocol emissions

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Compare formula output to intended economic design

threats.title: Permanent NFT Lock via Timestamp Underflow in Dirty Plot Handling
threats.hypothesis_description: When a landlord's plot count decreases, a renter's plot becomes invalid. _farmPlots attempts to cap rewards using plotMetadata[landlord].lastUpdated. If this timestamp is older than the renter's lastToilDate, the calculation underflows and reverts, permanently locking the NFT.
threats.investigation_rationale: Because unstakeMunchable forces a call to _farmPlots, any revert in _farmPlots completely bricks the unstake function, resulting in a permanent loss of the user's NFT.
preconditions: Renter has farmed recently (lastToilDate is recent)
Landlord's plot count decreases, invalidating the renter's plotId
Landlord's lastUpdated timestamp is older than renter's lastToilDate

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Landlord decreases locked value without updating plot metadata, trapping renter NFTs
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Griefing or accidental permanent loss of user assets

economic_questions: Can landlords intentionally trigger this to trap specific renters?
Does LockManager update plotMetadata automatically on unlock?
validation_requirements: Simulate landlord unlock without plotMetadata update
Attempt to unstake renter NFT and verify underflow revert

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Landlord must have locked value to unlock

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: After renter farms but before they unstake

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Total loss of staked Munchable NFT
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Value of all NFTs staked on volatile landlords

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: LockManager unlock flow and AccountManager updatePlotMetadata triggers

threats.title: Unbacked Yield Accrual via Dirty Flag Off-By-One Error
threats.hypothesis_description: The boundary check for invalid plots uses a strict less-than operator (_getNumPlots < plotId). Since plotId is 0-indexed, when a landlord's plot count exactly equals the plotId, the plot is invalid but the dirty flag is not triggered, allowing unbacked yield accrual.
threats.investigation_rationale: This allows tokens to continue minting rewards on plots that are no longer backed by the landlord's locked value, inflating the reward supply and violating the core economic invariant of the protocol.
preconditions: Renter is staked on plotId N
Landlord reduces locked value such that _getNumPlots returns exactly N

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: 4 < 4 is false, so dirty flag is bypassed for invalid plotId 4
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Landlord and renter collude: landlord unlocks value, renter continues earning full yield on unbacked plot
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Extraction of protocol rewards without providing the required economic security (locked value)

economic_questions: How much unbacked yield can be generated this way?
Can this be scaled across multiple accounts?
validation_requirements: Stake on plot N, reduce landlord plots to N, call farmPlots
Verify rewards are minted without dirty flag capping

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Initial capital to lock, which is then withdrawn

threats.title: Infinite Schnibbles Minting via Negative Bonus Underflow
threats.hypothesis_description: If the combined realm and rarity bonuses result in a finalBonus <= -100, the reward calculation numerator becomes negative. Casting this negative int256 to uint256 causes a massive underflow, minting near-infinite Schnibbles.
threats.investigation_rationale: While dependent on the configuration of REALM_BONUSES and RARITY_BONUSES, if any combination allows a penalty of -100% or worse, it completely destroys the protocol's reward economy.
deployment_assumptions: REALM_BONUSES or RARITY_BONUSES contain values that can sum to <= -100
RARITY_BONUSES uint8 values > 127 are cast to negative int8

proof_of_signal.pattern: uint256((int256(...) + ...) / 100) with no check for negative result

preconditions: Attacker possesses an NFT with attributes that map to a combined bonus <= -100

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Negative int256 cast to uint256 wraps to ~2^256
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Attacker stakes specific NFT and farms to trigger underflow
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker receives ~2^256 Schnibbles, effectively infinite protocol rewards

economic_questions: What are the exact values deployed in REALM_BONUSES and RARITY_BONUSES?
Does the uint8 to int8 cast for RARITY_BONUSES create unintended negative values?

potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All protocol value tied to Schnibbles

threats.title: Complete Protocol DoS via Incorrect Configuration Storage Keys
threats.hypothesis_description: The _reconfigure function loads numeric configuration values (like PRICE_PER_PLOT and MIN_TAX_RATE) using StorageKeys intended for contract addresses (e.g., StorageKey.LockManager). This likely results in loading incorrect values or zeros.
threats.investigation_rationale: If PRICE_PER_PLOT loads as 0, _getNumPlots will revert with a division by zero error, bricking all staking, unstaking, and farming operations across the entire protocol.
deployment_assumptions: ConfigStorage returns 0 for address keys when queried via getUint, or returns the integer representation of the address
threats.config_evidence: LandManager.sol lines 50-64 uses StorageKey.LockManager for MIN_TAX_RATE
preconditions: Contract is initialized or reconfigured with the current code

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Division by zero in _getNumPlots bricks all core functions

economic_questions: How does ConfigStorage handle getUint for address keys in production?
validation_requirements: Check ConfigStorage implementation and deployed state

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Inability to stake, unstake, or farm

threats.title: Staking Limit Bypass via Off-By-One Error
threats.hypothesis_description: The staking limit check uses strictly greater than (> 10) instead of greater than or equal to (>= 10). This allows a user to stake 11 Munchables instead of the intended maximum of 10.
threats.investigation_rationale: While the economic impact is limited to one extra NFT per account, it is a clear code defect that violates the intended protocol constraints and allows users to bypass staking caps.
preconditions: User attempts to stake an 11th Munchable

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: 10 > 10 is false, allowing the 11th push to succeed
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: User stakes 11 NFTs to maximize yield beyond intended account limits
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: 10% more yield per account than the protocol design intended

economic_questions: Does the extra NFT cause any out-of-gas issues in loops?
validation_requirements: Attempt to stake 12th NFT and verify revert

potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Yield of 1 NFT per maxed account
